narratives.evidence_quote: अमेरिका और ब्रिटेन पर होगा परमाणु हमला
narratives.reasoning: The headline and repeated statements in the article explicitly evoke fear by predicting a nuclear attack on the US and UK, directly amplifying anxieties about severe escalation and catastrophic consequences.

narratives.narrative_name: URW: Blaming the war on others rather than the invader
narratives.evidence_quote: यूक्रेन आक्रामक मूड में है और अमेरिका और ब्रिटेन की मदद से रूस पर ड्रोन हमले कर रहा है.
narratives.reasoning: This statement frames Ukraine as the aggressor ('aggressive mood') and highlights Western support for attacks on Russia, shifting the narrative of responsibility away from Russia's invasion and onto Ukraine and its allies.

narratives.evidence_quote: यूक्रेन को रूस पर हमले के लिए पश्चिमी देशों द्वारा आपूर्ति किए गए हथियारों का उपयोग करने की इजाजत देने का मतलब होगा कि रूस और नाटो के बीच युद्ध चल रहा है.
narratives.reasoning: This quote portrays Russia as being forced into a conflict with NATO due to Western actions (supplying weapons to Ukraine), framing Russia as reacting to external provocation and positioning itself as a victim of Western escalation.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Amplifying war-related fears: There is a real possibility that nuclear weapons will be employed
subnarratives.evidence_quote: अमेरिका और ब्रिटेन पर होगा परमाणु हमला
subnarratives.reasoning: The headline directly states that a nuclear attack will happen on the US and UK, evoking fear about the use of nuclear weapons.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Amplifying war-related fears: Russia will also attack other countries
subnarratives.evidence_quote: पुतिन ने कहा कि अगर ये देश यूक्रेन को रूस पर हमला करने देंगे तो वे उन देशों पर हमला कर देंगे.
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote explicitly states Putin's threat to attack other countries (the US and UK) if they allow Ukraine to attack Russia, promoting the idea that Russia will attack other countries.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: चेतावनी में कहा गया था कि यूक्रेन को रूस पर हमले के लिए पश्चिमी देशों द्वारा आपूर्ति किए गए हथियारों का उपयोग करने की इजाजत देने का मतलब होगा कि रूस और नाटो के बीच युद्ध चल रहा है.
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement frames Western support for Ukraine as potentially triggering a direct war between Russia and NATO, amplifying fears of a broader, direct conflict. It supports the parent narrative of amplifying war-related fears but does not fit the specific definitions of the other subnarratives (e.g., it is not a claim that NATO *will* intervene, but a warning that support could be interpreted as war).

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Blaming the war on others rather than the invader: The West are the aggressors
subnarratives.evidence_quote: यूक्रेन आक्रामक मूड में है और अमेरिका और ब्रिटेन की मदद से रूस पर ड्रोन हमले कर रहा है।
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly shifts the responsibility for the conflict's escalation and aggression onto Ukraine, which is portrayed as being in an 'aggressive mood' and attacking Russia with the help of Western nations (America and Britain). It frames the West as enabling and supporting the attacks, thus blaming them for the war's continuation and severity.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Blaming the war on others rather than the invader: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: चेतावनी में कहा गया था कि यूक्रेन को रूस पर हमले के लिए पश्चिमी देशों द्वारा आपूर्ति किए गए हथियारों का उपयोग करने की इजाजत देने का मतलब होगा कि रूस और नाटो के बीच युद्ध चल रहा है।
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote supports the parent narrative by framing the potential for a wider war as a consequence of Western actions (supplying weapons and permitting their use), thereby shifting blame for escalation onto the West. However, it does not fit the specific 'Ukraine is the aggressor' subnarrative (as it focuses on Western permission/supply) nor does it perfectly match the 'The West are the aggressors' subnarrative (which is already captured by the first quote). It is a distinct argument that supports blaming the war on others, constituting a remainder.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Russia is the Victim: Russia actions in Ukraine are only self-defence
subnarratives.evidence_quote: पुतिन ने आगे कहा कि परमाणु हथियारों के उपयोग की शर्तों को स्पष्ट करने वाला यह परिवर्तन पुतिन द्वारा अमेरिका और दूसरी उत्तरी अटलांटिक संधि संगठन (नाटो) सहयोगियों को दी गई चेतावनी के बाद किया गया है. चेतावनी में कहा गया था कि यूक्रेन को रूस पर हमले के लिए पश्चिमी देशों द्वारा आपूर्ति किए गए हथियारों का उपयोग करने की इजाजत देने का मतलब होगा कि रूस और नाटो के बीच युद्ध चल रहा है.
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote frames Russia's actions (including the threat of nuclear response) as a direct consequence of Western/NATO actions enabling Ukraine to attack Russia. It presents Russia's stance as purely reactive and defensive against an attack facilitated by the West, aligning with the self-defence justification.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Russia is the Victim: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: यूक्रेन आक्रामक मूड में है और अमेरिका और ब्रिटेन की मदद से रूस पर ड्रोन हमले कर रहा है.
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement portrays Ukraine as the aggressor actively attacking Russia with Western help, casting Russia in the role of the victim under attack. It supports the parent narrative of Russia being a victim but does not fit neatly into the more specific subnarratives about Russophobia or Ukrainian extremism.
